summary: W852 now routes one W732 route-data provider through the selected-cochain branch into the canonical direct-localization stable-instance boundary and exposes that package at the bounded-derived and original-four-task product-data surfaces. This is a source patch checkpoint only: the original positive witness remains blocked because no concrete nullary W732 route-data provider or nullary direct-localization boundary input has been constructed.
known_gaps: No declaration currently defines OriginalFourTaskProductSuccess or originalFourTaskProductSuccess.
OriginalFourTaskProductData still requires caller-supplied bounded-derived stable data or W852 route data; no nullary product witness exists.
A nullary Dbounded.MetrizableStableInstanceBoundaryDirectLocalizationInputs value is still not constructible from current source.
A concrete MetrizableWppBoundaryRelationTargetClosednessNormalizedDataProviderW732 value is still not constructible from current source.
Existing direct-left-calculus declarations remain parameterized by route data.
The direct-localization boundary still needs WPP transfer inputs and a source-side direct-localization triangulated core.
remaining_work: Construct a concrete nullary W732 route-data provider or a nullary direct-localization boundary input.
Use that source data to build a nullary bounded-derived stable package.
Assemble and audit the final original four-task positive witness.
Build the Tier C review packet and only then permit update_goal complete.
verified_commands: jq -r '.packages[] | select(.name=="mathlib" or .name=="aesop" or .name=="Qq" or .name=="batteries" or .name=="Cli") | [.name,.inputRev,.rev] | @tsv' lake-manifest.json
